bugs.description: The algorithm does not guarantee that every non‑empty substring occurs an odd number of times. For odd n it prints a string of all 'a's, which yields even occurrence counts for many substrings (e.g., length‑2 substrings).
bugs.expected_output: Any string of length 3 where each substring appears an odd number of times, e.g. "abc".
bugs.actual_behavior: The program outputs "aaa". In this string the substring "aa" occurs 2 times, which is even, violating the requirement.

bugs.description: For even n greater than 2 the constructed string (n‑1 times 'a' followed by 'b') also fails the condition because substrings consisting solely of 'a's of length 2 appear an even number of times.
bugs.expected_output: Any valid string of length 4, e.g. "abca".
bugs.actual_behavior: The program outputs "aaab". The substring "aa" occurs twice (positions 1‑2 and 2‑3), which is even, violating the requirement.

bugs.description: The algorithm does not guarantee that every non‑empty substring occurs an odd number of times. For odd n it prints a string of all 'a's, which yields even occurrence counts for many substrings (e.g., length‑2 substrings).
bugs.expected_output: Any string of length 3 where each substring appears an odd number of times, e.g. "abc".
bugs.actual_behavior: The program outputs "aaa". In this string the substring "aa" occurs 2 times, which is even, violating the requirement.

bugs.expected_output: Any valid string of length 4, e.g. "abca".
bugs.actual_behavior: The program outputs "aaab". The substring "aa" occurs twice (positions 1‑2 and 2‑3), which is even, violating the requirement.

bugs.bug_description: The algorithm does not guarantee that every non‑empty substring occurs an odd number of times. For odd n it prints a string of all 'a's, which yields even occurrence counts for many substrings (e.g., length‑12 substrings).
bugs.reasoning: For odd n the output is "a" repeated n times. Any substring consisting of k consecutive 'a's occurs n‑k+1 times. With n odd, n‑k+1 has parity opposite to k. Hence for odd k the occurrence count is even (e.g., the substring "aa" when n=3 occurs 2 times). Therefore the requirement is violated.

bugs.bug_description: For even n greater than 2 the constructed string (n‑1 times 'a' followed by 'b') also fails the condition because substrings consisting solely of 'a's of length 2 appear an even number of times.
bugs.reasoning: For even n the output is "a" repeated (n‑1) times followed by "b". Consider a substring of k consecutive 'a's (k ≤ n‑1). Its number of occurrences is (n‑1)‑k+1 = n‑k. Since n is even, n‑k is even when k is odd and odd when k is even. Thus for k=2 (even) the count is odd, but for k=1 (odd) the count is even, and for k=3 (odd) the count is even as well. For example, with n=4 the string "aaab" contains the substring "aa" twice (positions 1‑2 and 2‑3), an even count. Hence the condition is not satisfied.

bugs.description: The algorithm does not guarantee that every non‑empty substring occurs an odd number of times. For odd n it prints a string of all 'a's, which yields even occurrence counts for many substrings (e.g., the substring "aa" when n=3 occurs 2 times).
bugs.fix: Replace the all‑'a' construction with a string that ensures each substring appears an odd number of times. One simple correct construction is to output the first n characters of the periodic sequence "abc" (i.e., s[i] = chr('a' + i % 3)). If n % 3 == 0, change the last character to 'd' to avoid an even count of the single‑character substrings. This guarantees that every substring occurs exactly once (odd) for all n.
bugs.reasoning: For odd n the output is "a" repeated n times. Any substring consisting of k consecutive 'a's occurs n‑k+1 times. With n odd, n‑k+1 has parity opposite to k. Hence for odd k the occurrence count is even (e.g., the substring "aa" when n=3 occurs 2 times). Therefore the requirement is violated.

bugs.description: For even n greater than 2 the constructed string (n‑1 times 'a' followed by 'b') also fails the condition because substrings consisting solely of 'a's of length 2 appear an even number of times.
bugs.fix: Use the same corrected construction as above: output the first n characters of the periodic sequence "abc" and, when n % 3 == 0, replace the final character with 'd'. This yields a string where every substring occurs exactly once (odd) for any even n as well.
bugs.reasoning: For even n the output is "a" repeated (n‑1) times followed by "b". Consider a substring of k consecutive 'a's (k ≤ n‑1). Its number of occurrences is (n‑1)‑k+1 = n‑k. Since n is even, n‑k is even when k is odd and odd when k is even. Thus for k=1 (odd) the count is even, and for k=3 (odd) the count is even as well. For example, with n=4 the string "aaab" contains the substring "aa" twice (positions 1‑2 and 2‑3), an even count. Hence the condition is not satisfied.
